easy.title: Create an FAQ Page with Sections
easy.description: Build an FAQ page that uses at least 3 <section> elements, each grouping related questions. Each section must have a heading. Use proper heading hierarchy (h2 for sections, h3 for questions inside each section).
easy.starterCode: Frequently Asked Questions
easy.solution:
Frequently Asked Questions

General Questions

What is this service?

We provide web development education and resources.

How do I get started?

Create an account and browse our course catalog.

Account Questions

How do I reset my password?

Click 'Forgot Password' on the login page and follow the email instructions.

Can I delete my account?

Yes, go to Account Settings and select 'Delete Account'.

Billing Questions

What payment methods do you accept?

We accept Visa, Mastercard, and PayPal.

Can I get a refund?

We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee on all plans.

medium.title: Landing Page with Accessible Sections
medium.description: Build a marketing landing page with at least 4 sections (Hero, Features, Testimonials, Pricing). Each section must become a proper ARIA landmark using aria-labelledby. Include a navigation menu that scrolls to each section. Style the layout with CSS Grid.
medium.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Product Landing Page</title>
  <style>
    /* Add responsive grid styles */
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <header>
    <nav>
      <!-- Add navigation links to each section -->
    </nav>
  </header>
  <main>
    <!-- Hero section -->
    <!-- Features section -->
    <!-- Testimonials section -->
    <!-- Pricing section -->
  </main>
</body>
</html>

hard.title: Full Documentation Page with Nested Sections and Table of Contents
hard.description: Build a documentation-style page with deeply nested sections, a sticky table of contents generated from the section structure, and proper ARIA landmarks. The page should have at least 3 levels of nesting and demonstrate the correct heading hierarchy.
hard.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Documentation</title>
  <style>
    /* Two-column layout: sidebar TOC + content */
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <!-- Two-column layout with table of contents and documentation content -->
</body>
</html>
hard.solution:
Contents

  • 1. Introduction
    • 1.1 What is Section?
    • 1.2 Why Use Section?
  • 2. Usage Guide
    • 2.1 Basic Syntax
    • 2.2 Nesting Sections
      • 2.2.1 Two Levels
      • 2.2.2 Three Levels
  • 3. Best Practices

The Section Element Documentation

1. Introduction

1.1 What is Section?

The <section> element represents a standalone section of content that is thematically related.

1.2 Why Use Section?

Sections improve accessibility, SEO, and code maintainability by providing semantic structure.

2. Usage Guide

2.1 Basic Syntax

<section>
  <h2>Section Title</h2>
  <p>Content...</p>
</section>

2.2 Nesting Sections

2.2.1 Two Levels

Nest sections with proper heading hierarchy.

2.2.2 Three Levels

Deeper nesting is possible but avoid excessive depth.

3. Best Practices

  • Always include a heading
  • Use aria-labelledby for landmark regions
  • Maintain proper heading hierarchy
  • Don't use sections for styling hooks
